articles.title: What to know about Tiger Woods’ rollover crash in Florida
articles.description:
Tiger Woods’ refusal to take a urine test following his recent rollover crash can be prosecuted under a change to Florida law last year.

The post What to know about Tiger Woods’ rollover crash in Florida appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Tiger Woods refused a urine test after his rollover crash in Florida.
Florida law change allows prosecution for refusal to take a urine test.
Boston residents may see increased awareness of DUI laws and consequences.

articles.title: Airport bottlenecks ease as TSA workers get paid, but shutdown continues
articles.description:
The Transportation Safety Administration says most of its officers received most of their backpay Monday for working during the shutdown.

The post Airport bottlenecks ease as TSA workers get paid, but shutdown continues appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: TSA officers received backpay on March 30, easing airport bottlenecks in Boston.
Most TSA workers were paid for their shutdown work, improving security lines.
Boston travelers can expect shorter wait times at Logan Airport due to staffing relief.

articles.title: Man who crashed pickup into Michigan synagogue was inspired by Iran-backed Hezbollah, FBI says
articles.description:
The FBI says a man who crashed his pickup truck into a Detroit-area synagogue earlier in March was carrying out an attack inspired by Iran-backed militant group Hezbollah and sought to inflict as much damage as possible.

The post Man who crashed pickup into Michigan synagogue was inspired by Iran-backed Hezbollah, FBI says appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Man crashed pickup into Detroit-area synagogue in March, inspired by Hezbollah.
FBI reports the attacker aimed to inflict maximum damage during the incident.
Boston residents should remain vigilant against potential similar attacks influenced by extremist groups.

articles.title: Celtics get good news on Jaylen Brown’s Achilles tendinitis injury
articles.description:
Jaylen Brown is expected to be back on the hardwood Monday night as the Celtics take on the Hawks.

The post Celtics get good news on Jaylen Brown’s Achilles tendinitis injury  appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Jaylen Brown is expected to return to play on March 30 against the Hawks.
Brown's Achilles tendinitis injury has sidelined him for recent games.
His return boosts Celtics' chances in the playoff race for Boston fans.

articles.title: Major pharmaceutical company to cut nearly 250 Massachusetts jobs
articles.description:
The layoffs by Cambridge-based Takeda, affecting its Kendall Square workforce, are part of a restructuring plan to save $1.25 billion annually by 2028.

The post Major pharmaceutical company to cut nearly 250 Massachusetts jobs appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Takeda Pharmaceuticals will lay off nearly 250 employees in Cambridge's Kendall Square.
The layoffs are part of a restructuring plan aiming to save $1.25 billion by 2028.
Local job market may be affected as Takeda reduces its workforce significantly.
